content: Metformin is the first-line medication for Type 2 Diabetes. It works by making your body cells more sensitive to insulin, allowing glucose to be used more effectively. It also reduces the amount of sugar produced by your liver. When used alone, it rarely causes hypoglycemia, making it a very safe foundational drug.

In the first week or two of starting Metformin, about 1 in 3 people experience stomach discomfort, such as nausea, bloating, or diarrhea. This is completely normal. To minimize this, please ensure you take the pill 【during or immediately after a meal】. Most symptoms will resolve within a few days as your body adjusts.

Managing diabetes is a team effort involving diet and exercise. When visiting a Hawker Centre, try to ask for 'Siew Dai' (less sugar) and less gravy. You can also aim for a brisk walk around your HDB estate or visit an ActiveSG gym. Since you asked, I can 【generate a personalized 7-day Low-GI diet and exercise plan】 tailored to your current health status.

Amlodipine is a classic blood pressure medication. It works by relaxing the walls of your blood vessels, allowing them to widen. This reduces the pressure within the vessels, much like widening a pipe reduces water pressure. It is long-acting, meaning one pill provides stable control for the entire day.

When starting Amlodipine, the sudden widening of small blood vessels might cause mild facial flushing or a slight headache. Some patients also notice mild swelling around the ankles after standing for long periods. These are usually harmless. Elevating your feet during rest can help. If swelling becomes severe, we should inform your doctor to review the dosage.

Missing a dose when you're busy is very common, so don't be too hard on yourself. However, regular adherence is key to preventing drastic fluctuations in blood pressure or sugar. I can use the system API to 【schedule a medication alarm and voice reminder for tomorrow】 to help you build a consistent routine.

Atorvastatin helps lower cholesterol and stabilizes plaques in your blood vessels, acting as a 'security guard' for your arteries. However, there is a critical lifestyle rule: 【Do not consume large amounts of Grapefruit juice】 while on this medication. Grapefruit blocks the breakdown of the drug, which can lead to severe muscle pain and liver issues.

metadata.source: Institute of Mental Health (IMH) SG / Psychology

content: Getting a diagnosis and seeing multiple medication boxes can feel overwhelming and scary. It is completely natural to feel anxious and ask, 'Is my life going to be like this forever?' Please take a deep breath. These medications are proven to be safe 'health bodyguards' verified by HSA. We see this diagnosis as a signal to start loving your body more. Your clinic doctor and our system nurse will be with you every step of the way.

It can be extremely frustrating when you've worked hard on your diet and taken your meds, only to see your health indicators rise. Please know that this isn't your fault! The human body isn't a machine; things like poor sleep, work stress, or dehydration from Singapore's hot weather can affect your readings. Your past effort is NOT wasted. Let's stay calm and discuss a slight adjustment with your doctor during the next visit.

You have been prescribed 'Enteric-Coated Aspirin'. This is different from a normal painkiller; 【it MUST be taken on an empty stomach】 (at least 30-60 minutes before a meal). This allows the pill to bypass the stomach quickly and dissolve in the intestine, protecting your stomach lining. If you notice gum bleeding or black tarry stools, please contact your SingHealth pharmacist or doctor immediately.
